description_long: This is a rectangular, two-story apartment building with a flat roof and a weathered, utilitarian facade. The exterior walls are painted a faded off-white, showing signs of dirt and aging, with some areas appearing more stained than others. Each floor features a row of evenly spaced, rectangular windows with dark frames and visible grilles, suggesting a repetitive and functional design. There are no visible balconies or decorative elements, and the building's surface is largely unadorned except for some minor utility fixtures and markings. The structure appears to be made primarily of concrete or stucco, typical of mid- to late-20th-century urban residential blocks. The overall impression is of a practical, no-frills apartment building suited for dense urban environments.
description: A two-story, rectangular apartment building with a flat roof, faded off-white facade, and rows of evenly spaced windows. The exterior is weathered and utilitarian, with no balconies or decorative features.
description_view_0: Front view showing two rows of four windows each, with visible weathering and minor utility fixtures on the facade.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the building's very thin profile, with no windows or features visible.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying two rows of four windows and a similarly weathered surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the building's thin profile with no visible features.
attribute_window_arrangement: The windows are arranged in two horizontal rows, each row containing four evenly spaced windows, creating a symmetrical and repetitive pattern across the facade.
